tool_args.label: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the December 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Richard Davey, President, New York City Transit
Janno Lieber, MTA Chairman and CEO
Quemuel Arroyo, MTA Chief Accessibility Officer
Shanifah Reiarra, MTA Chief Customer Officer

payload.description: Transformative Light Rail Transit Project connecting communities in Brooklyn and Queens to 17 subway lines and the Long Island Railroad
Expanding to 3,600 customers, more invitations to eligible customers will be sent on a rolling basis
Expanded to more than 1,000 buses across 57 bus routes in all five boroughs

Wayfinding, language translation, and service information app for blind or low-vision customers, expanded to 72 St 1 2 3 and 66 St 1 stations, bus stop GARs along M23 SBS and M66 routes, with plan to expand to 40 stations in 2024

New wide aisle faregates in service at Atlantic Ave-Barclays Center and Sutphin-Archer E J Z Stations, with Sutphin featuring first fully modernized fare line; additional gates being installed at Astoria Blvd N W and Penn A C E stations

ABLE cameras expanded across network, bringing speed and safety improvements to a total of 21 routes; transitioning to ACE (Automated Camera Enforcement) in next year for double parked cars and cars in bus stops
15 Customer Service Centers opened, bringing resources like Reduced-Fare applications and OMNY support to communities; fifteenth and final center to open at 168 St A C 1
Goal of 50 stations renewed in 2023, on track to complete 53 Re-NEW-Vations

payload.raw_value_text: November paid weekday subway ridership averaged 3.78 million
November paid bus ridership dropped from 1.379 million to 1.294 million
97% of calls made to Paratransit's Command Center were answered
increase customer satisfaction to 70% on all modes by June 2024

tool_args.label: Paratransit Primary Carrier No-Show Rate November 2023

payload.raw_value_text: Broker service, the result for November 2023 was a 1.30 No-Show Rate

tool_args.label: New or Upgraded Bus Lane Miles 2023

payload.raw_value_text: NYC DOT has added 15.6 miles of new or upgraded bus lanes by the end of 2023
Monthly Fires for November 2023 (69) decreased when compared to the same month in the previous year (104)

tool_args.label: Wide-aisle fare gates opening at Sutphin Blvd-Archer Ave

payload.description: Opening of wide-aisle fare gates and new Customer Service Center at Sutphin Blvd - Archer Ave E J Z

tool_args.label: 7 Av F G Station Accessibility Upgrades

payload.description: New elevators and other ADA upgrades completed at 7 Av F G

warnings: event.stations_affected is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Station lists are external service/location context.

payload.description: New elevators and other ADA upgrades completed at 181 St A

warnings: event.stations_affected is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Station lists are external service/location context.

tool_args.label: G and C line frequency increase starting December 18

payload.description: Headways between trains reduced by two minutes on the G during midday hours, and on the C during evening hours
payload.event_name: G and C line frequency increase

payload.description: Final TransitTalk event of 2023 at Fordham Road in the Bronx, with President Davey, Department of Subways Stations Division, MTA Customer Services, MTA OMNY team, GCR and NYPD Transit Bureau
MTA held its last two of three in-person town hall meetings on Interborough Express in Elmhurst, Queens, and Sunset Park, Brooklyn
Yellow Detectable Warning Strips (DWS) installed on 52 platforms across 25 subway stations across Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, and the Bronx in 2022 and 2023
payload.location_text: 52 platforms across 25 subway stations across Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, and the Bronx

normalized_location.raw_text: 52 platforms across 25 subway stations across Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, and the Bronx

payload.claim_text: November paid weekday subway ridership averaged 3.78 million, in line with the October number (when accounting for seasonality), at 67% of pre-pandemic levels
payload.subject: subway ridership

payload.claim_text: November paid bus ridership dropped from 1.379 million to 1.294 million. This is a larger drop than usually expected due to seasonality. This was mostly due to the continued rise in bus fare evasion with preliminary numbers showing the evasion rate rising by an additional 1.3% in November
payload.subject: bus fare evasion

payload.claim_text: securing the financial future of our essential transit services by delivering on our commitment of $300 million in savings, starting in 2024
payload.subject: NYCT savings

payload.description: MTA held town hall meetings on Interborough Express in Elmhurst, Queens, and Sunset Park, Brooklyn

tool_args.label: Wide Aisle Fare Gates - Opening Event

payload.description: Wide-aisle fare gates opened at Sutphin Blvd-Archer Ave on December 4

tool_args.label: NYCT - Prepared for NYCT & Bus Committee

payload.description: Performance metrics document prepared for the December 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee
Open Stroller Pilot expanded to more than 1,000 buses across 57 bus routes
NYC DOT added 15.6 miles of new or upgraded bus lanes by end of 2023, improving bus speeds and service reliability

payload.missing_information: Customer Pulse Survey not conducted in November 2023 as its normal time 'in field' conflicted with the MTA's Fall 2023 Customers Count Study
payload.gap_text: There was no Pulse Survey in November 2023 as its normal time 'in field' conflicted with the MTA's Fall 2023 Customers Count Study

payload.missing_information: October and November 2023 fare evasion data not yet available for subway and bus ridership tables
payload.gap_text: October and November fare evasion data not yet available
